items.title: Farmers take to the stage to leave stress of the land behind
items.summary: Putting their livestock, tractors and drought feeding aside for a night, these farmers are taking to the stage.

items.title: Single mums are seeking each other out and turning to shared housing
items.summary: As rental and living costs surge, and as single mums remain one of the most vulnerable groups in the country, some are moving in together and finding many benefits.

items.title: Hull City promoted to EPL after beating Middlesbrough in EFL Championship playoff
items.summary: Riley McGree's Premier League dream is over and the Socceroos star will hope his World Cup hopes are not also dashed after limping off with a hamstring injury in Middlesbrough's Championship playoff final defeat to Hull City at Wembley.

items.title: Regional SA residents say power problems should have been fixed 18 months ago
items.summary: After losing hundreds of thousands of dollars due to power outages, Yorke Peninsula businesses say SA Power Networks' $10 million action plan should have come a long time ago.

items.title: Holes in Australia's sovereign capability exposed by Iran war supply chain issues

items.title: Five Italian divers died in a Maldives cave system. Authorities still don't know why they went down there
items.summary: The question of why experienced divers would descend to depths of more than 50 metres on a single tank of air — let alone enter a cave while doing so — baffles members of the relatively close-knit deep-sea diving community.

items.title: One Nation sets combative tone in South Australia, but tougher political tests await
items.summary: One Nation broke its parliamentary silence to talk about culture wars and political barneys. But not everything went as expected.

items.title: Labor has picked a fight on tax, but not all critics are vested interests

items.title: Rachel Ward gave up glamour for cows and a not-so-simple country life

items.title: Why Australia still struggles to build bike-friendly cities

items.title: ASIO assessment did not warn about heightened Hanukkah risks before Bondi attack
